info.title: Dummy example with all spec features included
info.description:
This is an example of AsyncAPI specification file that is supposed to include all possible features of the AsyncAPI specification. Do not use it on production.

It's goal is to support development of documentation and code generation with the [AsyncAPI Generator](https://github.com/asyncapi/generator/) and [Template projects](https://github.com/search?q=topic%3Aasyncapi+topic%3Agenerator+topic%3Atemplate).

development.description:
Private server that requires authorization.
Once the socket is open you can subscribe to private-data channels by sending an authenticated subscribe request message.

The API client must request an authentication "token" via the following REST API endpoint "GetWebSocketsToken" to connect to WebSockets Private endpoints. For more details read https://support.kraken.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034437672-How-to-retrieve-a-WebSocket-authentication-token-Example-code-in-Python-3
The resulting token must be provided in the "token" field of any new private WebSocket feed subscription: 
```json
{
  "event": "subscribe",
  "subscription":
  {
    "name": "ownTrades",
    "token": "WW91ciBhdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiB0b2tlbiBnb2VzIGhlcmUu"
  }
}
```

```elixir
defmodule Hello do
  def world do
    IO.puts("hello")
  end
end
```

security.description: Developer API key for the development environment. Use this key only for internal testing.

port.description:
Secure connection port used for MQTT connections.

Use `8883` for encrypted traffic and `8884` for alternate secure connections.

testing.description:
Testing/Staging MQTT broker for pre-production testing.

This environment closely mirrors the production setup but may experience interruptions during QA cycles.

security.description: Test API key for the testing environment. Ensure you do not use it in production.
OpenID Connect authentication for testing environment

port.description:
Secure connection port used for staging MQTT traffic.

Supports encryption over the listed ports.

region.description:
Server region to connect to for testing purposes.

Examples include regions like `us-east-1` for US East and `eu-west-1` for EU West.

production.description:
Private server that requires authorization.
Once the socket is open you can subscribe to private-data channels by sending an authenticated subscribe request message.

The API client must request an authentication "token" via the following REST API endpoint "GetWebSocketsToken" to connect to WebSockets Private endpoints. For more details read https://support.kraken.com/hc/en-us/articles/360034437672-How-to-retrieve-a-WebSocket-authentication-token-Example-code-in-Python-3
The resulting token must be provided in the "token" field of any new private WebSocket feed subscription: 
```json
{
  "event": "subscribe",
  "subscription":
  {
    "name": "ownTrades",
    "token": "WW91ciBhdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbiB0b2tlbiBnb2VzIGhlcmUu"
  }
}
```

```elixir
defmodule Hello do
  def world do
    IO.puts("hello")
  end
end
```

security.description: Production API key with rate limiting and enhanced security. Keep this key secure.
OpenID Connect authentication for production environment

port.description:
Secure connection port used for production MQTT traffic.

The default port `8883` is recommended for most use cases.

region.description:
Region of the server to connect to.

This allows for better latency and reliability depending on the geographical location.
